name: Natural selection core mechanism
question_text: Which of these best describes the mechanism of natural selection as proposed by Darwin and Wallace?
general_feedback: Natural selection requires heritable variation in a trait, a difference in survival or reproductive success linked to that variation, and transmission of the advantageous variants to the next generation. Over many generations allele frequencies shift.
correct_feedback: Correct — Variation + differential fitness + heredity drives natural selection.

answers.text: Heritable variants that improve survival or reproduction become more common over generations.
answers.feedback: Variation + differential fitness + heredity drives natural selection.

answers.text: Organisms consciously change their bodies to better fit their environment.
answers.feedback: That is Lamarckism (inheritance of acquired characteristics), which is not how natural selection works.

answers.text: All species steadily progress toward a predetermined goal of perfection.
answers.feedback: Evolution has no fixed target; it is a response to current selective pressures.

answers.text: Random mutations are always beneficial and quickly spread through a population.
answers.feedback: Most mutations are neutral or harmful; only those that increase fitness in the current environment tend to spread.
